rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory-building section, where the authors draw on dual-entitlement/effort literature to argue that consumers consider firm effort when deciding their own effort. That argument is then directly tied to hypothesis development about when firm conservation requests will trigger reactance or compliance. Although it is cited in a grouped citation, it serves a clear conceptual support role rather than a purely decorative background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2003) as part of the conceptual foundation that consumers evaluate their own effort relative to firm effort. This prior logic is carried into the hotel sustainability context to motivate predictions about consumer reactance and conservation behavior when firms ask customers to conserve resources.
